definitions.text: ISO 639-2 ISO 639-3 language code for Newar.

examples: This is a new scratch on my car! The band just released a new album.
The cookers cost £350 new but £150 secondhand.

definitions.text: Of recent origin; having taken place recently.
examples: I can't see you for a while; the pain is still too new. Did you see the new King Lear at the theatre?
We turned up some new evidence from the old files.

definitions.text: Synonym of newly, especially in composition.
examples: new-born, new-formed, new-found, new-mown hay, new-laid eggs
They are scraping the site clean to build new.
Out with the old, in with the new.

definitions.text: A typically light-coloured lager brewed by the bottom-fermentation method.
To make new; to recreate; to renew.
